data.narrative:
AnySignal is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised $34M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Planet Labs. Two valuation methods were applicable to derive a triangulated range.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $34M total raised proxy, yielding $68M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields $850M. The peer set ranges from $400M (ClearSpace and Hubble Network at Series A/B) to $2.0B (Cowboy Space Corporation at Series B), with a median of $850M. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple was not applicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $58M to $977M, with a median of $459M across the two applicable methods. The 16.8x spread between low and high reflects material uncertainty around stage progression timing, peer comparability weighting, and AnySignal's specific operational maturity relative to the sample. Key unknowns include revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and technical milestone trajectory—all of which would narrow this range materially with disclosure.
